AlertDefinition.AlertMessage: '${N=SwisEntity;M=Name}' thin AP managed by '${N=SwisEntity;M=ControllerName}' WLC goes down.
AlertDefinition.Description: This alert is triggered when a currently active thin access point (AP) goes down. The alert works with the AP primary-secondary WLC configuration. For more information, see https://www.solarwinds.com/documentation/kbloader.aspx?kb=SF26730.
AlertDefinition.Name: [Advanced] Thin AP Down

Condition.d5p2:Command: JOIN ( -- 1st table scope SELECT DISTINCT ap1.ID FROM ( SELECT api.ID, api.IndexValue, api.PreviousStatus, api.NodeID FROM ( SELECT ap.ID, wi.[Value] IndexValue, ap.PreviousStatus, ap.NodeID FROM Wireless_AccessPoints_View ap JOIN Wireless_Indexes wi ON wi.ID = ap.NodeID AND wi.[Index] = ap.[Index] WHERE ap.[Type] = 1 AND ap.[Status] = 2 ) api ) ap1 -- 1st table LEFT JOIN ( -- 2nd table scope SELECT api.ID, api.IndexValue, api.[Status], api.NodeID FROM ( SELECT ap.ID, wi.[Value] IndexValue, ap.[Status], ap.NodeID FROM Wireless_AccessPoints_View ap JOIN Wireless_Indexes wi ON wi.ID = ap.NodeID AND wi.[Index] = ap.[Index] WHERE ap.[Type] = 1 ) api ) ap2 -- 2nd table ON ap1.NodeID <> ap2.NodeID AND ap1.IndexValue = ap2.IndexValue WHERE ap2.ID IS NULL -- single AP OR (ap1.PreviousStatus <> 0) -- AP was not down then became down(part of primary-secondary WLC setup) OR (ap2.[Status] = 0) -- disappeared from primary/secondary WLC. So, we need to re-create alert ) disapAPs ON NPM_NV_WL_ALERT_APS_V.ID = disapAPs.ID

d2p1:ActionProperty.d2p1:PropertyValue: '${N=SwisEntity;M=Name}' thin AP managed by '${N=SwisEntity;M=ControllerName}' WLC goes down.

d2p1:ActionDefinition.d2p1:Description: Log the Alert in the Network Performance Monitor Event Log
d2p1:ActionDefinition.d2p1:Title: NetPerfMon Event Log : '${N=SwisEntity;M=Name}' thin AP managed by '${N=SwisEntity;M=ControllerName}' WLC goes down.
